title: One Small Reach This Week
subtitle: A weekly activity for closing a relational gap one move at a time
excerpt: A brief weekly activity for a relationship that has drifted. The practice asks you to identify one person and make one specific reach (a message, a question, a call) without waiting until the conditions are right.
sourceNotes: Draws on behavioural activation and social connection research.

practice.whenToUse: Use weekly as a small maintenance practice for a relationship that has drifted or one you want to keep warm. Not for acute conflict — for quiet distance.

content.text: A brief weekly activity for a relationship that has grown quiet. Relational drift tends to continue unless interrupted. This practice makes the interruption small enough to do without preparing for it.
New to activities as a practice? Read
Identify one person you have drifted from or one relationship you want to keep warm.

Choose one specific, small reach: a message saying you were thinking of them, a question about something they told you last time you spoke, a call you have been meaning to make. The reach needs to be a specific action, not a vague intention to get in touch.
Do it this week. Not when conditions are right. This week.

The reach does not need to open a big conversation or fix the distance. It needs to be a signal that the relationship is still in your attention. Small, consistent reaches do more than occasional large ones.
Draws on behavioural activation and social connection research.
